name: BET Surface Area Analysis
description: BET analysis measures specific surface area from multilayer gas physisorption: after degassing, nitrogen is dosed at increasing relative pressures, the BET equation is fit over its linear region to extract monolayer capacity, and the surface area follows from molecular cross-section.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Porous / powder sample"]
    N2["Degas + cool (LN2)"]
    N3["Dose N2 at varied P/P0"]
    N4["Measure adsorbed volume"]
    N5["Plot BET equation"]
    N6{"Linear in 0.05–0.3?"}
    N7["Narrow P/P0 window"]
    N8["Monolayer capacity Vm"]
    N9["Area = Vm·N·σ"]
    N10["Specific surface area"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|no| N7
    N7 -->|re-dose| N3
    N6 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

sources.title: Adsorption of gases in multimolecular layers (BET theory)
sources.authors: Brunauer, S.; Emmett, P. H.; Teller, E.
sources.journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society

sources.title: Physisorption of gases, with special reference to the evaluation of surface area (IUPAC technical report)
Introduction to Surface Chemistry and Catalysis, 2nd ed. — Surface Area
